mova.smallDescription: An app for personal trainers to move from scattered tools to one structured system for managing clients and workflows.
fullDescription: MOVA is a multi-tenant SaaS built end-to-end as a solo founder project. It replaces fragmented workflows based on spreadsheets, WhatsApp and manual PDFs, consolidating client management, training, nutrition and progress tracking into a single mobile system, built around real-world use cases and highly customizable workflows.
Designed through direct collaboration with personal trainers, translating real workflows into scalable product decisions.
mova.challenge: Personal trainers lose 3–4 hours daily managing clients through spreadsheets, messaging apps and disconnected PDFs. Existing tools are too generic and fail to reflect how they actually work, lacking the flexibility to handle real-world details like custom nutrition setups, local food brands, or adaptable training structures, forcing trainers to rely on manual workarounds.
mova.solution: Built MOVA as a mobile-first, multi-tenant SaaS from scratch, designed around how personal trainers actually work. The platform consolidates client management, training, nutrition, progress tracking and finance management into a single system, with highly customizable workflows that support real-world needs like custom nutrition setups and flexible training structures, all within a structured and scalable platform.

features.title: Client Management
features.description: Full client profiles with history, goals, physical data and progress over time, providing a complete overview of each client.

features.title: Training Plan Builder
features.description: Create structured training plans with exercises, sets, reps and rest, including alternative exercise options and flexible scheduling tailored to each client.

features.title: Advanced Nutrition System
features.description: Custom meal plans with support for local food brands, flexible meal structures and automatic nutritional calculations tailored to each client.

features.title: Client–Trainer Matching & Onboarding
features.description: Structured onboarding for trainers and clients, with a request-based system where clients choose trainers and trainers accept or decline requests.

features.title: Financial Management
features.description: Track payments, subscriptions and client-related finances, giving trainers visibility over their business.

features.title: Multi-tenant Architecture
features.description: Each trainer operates within an isolated workspace, with Row-Level Security ensuring secure data separation between accounts.

metrics.label: Built end-to-end as a solo founder
Estimated reduction in plan creation time

elephai.smallDescription: Rescued and rebuilt a pre-launch Flutter app, transforming an unstable codebase into a production-ready product in under 8 weeks.
fullDescription: ElephAi is an AI-powered mobile app focused on proactive emotional wellbeing, helping users detect and manage emotional distress before it escalates through real-time support and behavioural analysis.

I joined as Lead Developer during a critical pre-launch phase, taking full ownership of the app. I refactored the codebase, established a clean architecture, and delivered the first successful public release to both App Store and Google Play.
elephai.challenge: The app had been in development for months but was nowhere near production-ready. The codebase had no architectural boundaries, deeply coupled modules, and no clear ownership. The team needed someone to take the wheel, assess the damage, and ship.
elephai.solution: Took full technical ownership from day one. Audited the entire codebase, identified critical failure points, and rebuilt core modules with clear separation of concerns. Established architectural standards, documented decisions, and coordinated the App Store / Google Play release pipeline, delivering a stable, public-ready app in under 8 weeks.

features.title: Full Codebase Refactor
features.description: Rebuilt 100% of core modules, eliminating tightly coupled dependencies and establishing a scalable, maintainable architecture.

features.title: Architecture Ownership
features.description: Defined and documented all technical decisions, folder structure, state management patterns and API integration standards for the project.

features.title: Production Release Delivery
features.description: Set up and managed the App Store and Google Play release pipeline, delivering the app’s first successful public launch.

features.title: UI Implementation & Redesign
features.description: Implemented the new product design across the app, translating updated UI/UX into a consistent and  experience.

features.title: Ongoing Feature Development
features.description: Continuing to build new product features and integrations (including AI API) while maintaining architectural quality and velocity.

nearsoft-onboarding.smallDescription: Configurable digital onboarding platform deployed across multiple financial institutions, replacing manual workflows with a fully automated, CMS-driven process.
fullDescription: Built at Nearsoft, a fintech software company based in Madeira, this platform digitizes the entire client onboarding journey for banks and financial institutions. The system is multi-bank, white-label ready, and CMS-driven, allowing non-technical teams to configure onboarding flows, update content, and manage approval steps without writing code. I owned the frontend architecture and the Directus CMS integration end-to-end.
nearsoft-onboarding.challenge: Financial institutions relied on paper forms, manual data entry and fragmented internal tools, creating bottlenecks, compliance risks and poor customer experience. The platform needed to be robust enough for regulated environments, flexible enough to support multiple institutions, and fast enough for users to complete onboarding quickly from any device.
nearsoft-onboarding.solution: Built a configurable frontend architecture using React, Next.js and TypeScript, powered by a Directus CMS (REST + GraphQL). Onboarding flows are fully driven by dynamic configuration, enabling institutions to customize processes without code changes and reducing setup time from days to minutes. Implemented browser APIs for document capture, step persistence and real-time validation, improving completion rates and minimizing user drop-off.

features.title: CMS-Driven Onboarding System
features.description: Architected the onboarding platform around CMS-driven configuration using Directus, allowing flows, validation rules and content to be dynamically defined and adapted per institution without requiring code changes.

features.title: KYC & Document Processing
features.description: Implemented document capture flows and UI for identity verification processes, contributing to a fully digital KYC onboarding experience.

features.title: Multi-Institution Flow Support
features.description: Implemented onboarding flows adaptable to different institutional requirements, supporting variations in structure, validation and user scenarios across deployments.

features.title: Configurable Frontend Architecture
features.description: Designed and implemented a scalable frontend architecture driven by dynamic configuration, translating product designs into responsive and pixel-accurate interfaces.

features.title: State Persistence & Recovery
features.description: Built persistent onboarding state across sessions using browser-native APIs, allowing users to resume multi-step flows at any point without losing progress.

nearsoft-llm-chatbot.smallDescription: On-premise AI chatbot built with Ollama and Llama 3 — connecting private APIs and CMS data sources to automate internal information retrieval with zero cloud exposure.
nearsoft-llm-chatbot.fullDescription: Most companies send their internal data to third-party AI APIs — exposing sensitive information to the cloud. This prototype, built during my time at Nearsoft, took the opposite approach: a fully local LLM stack using Ollama and Llama 3, running entirely on company infrastructure. I designed and built the complete system — from the Express.js API layer connecting to private CMS and internal data sources, to the React chat interface where teams could query company knowledge in real time. It wasn't just a demo. It proved that enterprise-grade AI tooling is possible without a single byte leaving the building.
nearsoft-llm-chatbot.challenge: Internal teams were losing significant time manually searching across disconnected tools — private APIs, CMS platforms, internal docs — to find information that should have been instantly accessible. Off-the-shelf AI solutions were either too expensive, too generic, or required sending proprietary data to external cloud providers, which was a non-starter in a regulated industry context.
nearsoft-llm-chatbot.solution: Designed and built a full-stack AI chatbot running 100% on-premise using Ollama to serve Llama 3 locally. Built an Express.js backend that aggregated data from private APIs and CMS platforms into a unified context layer fed to the LLM. Built the frontend conversation interface with React, handling real-time streaming responses, conversation state, and low-latency UX. The result: a working internal knowledge assistant that demonstrated local LLMs are viable for enterprise workflows — with no cloud dependency, no data exposure, and no per-query cost.

features.title: Fully On-Premise LLM Stack
features.description: Ollama serves Llama 3 locally on company hardware — no API keys, no cloud costs, no data leaving the building. Validated that production-quality LLM inference is achievable without external dependencies.

features.title: Private API & CMS Aggregation
features.description: Express.js backend connects to internal APIs and CMS platforms, aggregating fragmented data sources into a unified context layer. The chatbot answers questions that previously required manual lookup across multiple tools.

features.title: Real-Time Streaming Chat Interface
features.description: React frontend with streaming response support, conversation history management, and optimistic UI patterns — ensuring a fast, fluid experience even when querying large internal datasets.

features.title: Low-Latency Response Architecture
features.description: API integration logic was designed for reliability and speed — minimizing round-trip time between the frontend, the Express layer, and the local LLM. Validated response quality and latency under realistic usage conditions.

features.title: Enterprise AI Feasibility Proof
features.description: The prototype directly demonstrated that local LLMs can replace cloud AI in internal tooling contexts — laying the technical and conceptual groundwork for future AI-enabled workflows within the company.

features.title: Multi-Source Context Injection
features.description: Engineered a context-building pipeline that pulls relevant data from multiple internal sources and injects it into the LLM prompt — enabling accurate, context-aware answers grounded in real company data.
